bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Rigo was born with a rare congenital disorder known as arthrogryposis that causes his muscles to stiffen and develop abnormally
  • Son of Robert Rigo and Celena Destefano
  • Named the 2015 Non-High School Male Athlete of the Year by the Spokane Youth Sports Commission in Washington
  • Enjoys sports, hunting and building
  • Member of Youth Outdoors Unlimited where he was certified with hunter education in 2013
  • At 14, Rigo was selected as the youngest member of Team USA for the 2013 IPC World Championships
  • His passion for the sport began in 2007 after joined ParaSport Spokane and met coach Teresa Skinner
Paralympic Experience
  • 1-time Paralympian
    • Paralympic Games Tokyo 2020, 7th (100m T52 - Men, 1500m T52 - Men, 400m T52 - Men)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2019 – 5th (1500m T52 - Men, 400m T52 - Men), 9th (100m T52 - Men)
  • Years of participation: 100m T52 - Men 2013, 2019; 1500m T52 - Men 2013, 2015, 2019; 400m T52 - Men 2015, 2019; 200m T52 - Men 2013
  • Top finish: 5th – 2013 (1500m T52 - Men), 2019 (1500m T52 - Men), 2019 (400m T52 - Men)

Quick Facts
  • Son of Ralph Riley and Kathi Peacock
  • Married Kendra Welling in 2015
  • Underwent surgery for Haglund’s syndrome in 2018
  • Works as a test-prep tutor.
Career Highlights

  • 2019 Chicago Marathon, 9th
  • 2019 USATF 20 km Road Running Championships, 15th
  • 2016 U.S. Olympic Team Trials, 15th (marathon)
  • 2015 USATF 10 Mile Road Running Championship, 7th
  • 2014 Chicago Marathon, 11th
  • Eight-time All-American at Stanford University
Olympic Experience
  • 1-time Olympian
    • Olympic Games Tokyo 2020, 29th (Marathon - Men)

USA Track and Field

Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Jeremy and Leah Rinehart
  • Has two older siblings: Brennan Rinehart and Aerial Carillo
  • Started diving in a summer league at age 7 and by age 10, she was diving year-round
  • Has also competed in swimming, gymnastics and cross country
  • Her favorite dive is 205C on 3-meter
  • Has to have her hair French braided with a braided flipped under a bun for competitions
  • Likes to listen to pop music, including AJR, Eminem, Olivia Rodriguo, Juice WRLD, and Machine Gun Kelly, while at meets
  • Favorite divers are Michael Hixon and Andrew Capobianco
  • Enjoys writing, journaling, and outdoor activities
  • Would like to become an author (she still has a book she wrote in kindergarten)
Career Highlights
  • 2021 Junior Pan American Championships team member (6th, 3-meter)

Quick Facts

  • If she wasn’t a professional tennis player, she would have gone into medicine.

Quick Facts
  • Personal: Daughter of Robert and Sharon Rittenhouse
  • Personal: Has four older siblings: Rob, Ivan, Ryan and Myra
  • Personal: Comes from an Olympic background
  • Personal: Her mom is a member of the International Swimming Hall of Fame
  • Personal: Sharon won a silver medal in the 1964 Olympics in the 400 IM, held nine world records and won gold at the 1963 Pan Am Games
  • Personal: Ariel's uncle Mike Finneran, Sharon's brother, was a diver in the 1972 Olympic Games, and her grandmother, Carolyn Finneran, was a team manager for the 1976 Olympic swim team
  • Personal: Ariel started diving in 1998
  • Personal: Enjoys surfing, dancing, reading and cooking
  • Personal: Favorite movie is "Gladiator."

  • Earned 72 national championship titles across four disciplines: road, track, cyclocross, and mountain bike
  • First ever American, Male or Female, to win the Tour of Flanders, also known as De Ronde Van Vlaanderen
  • One of her most pivotal experiences was going to college and racing collegiately
  • Her favorite post-ride meal is bacon and eggs
  • Her favorite quote is “what you do in life echoes in eternity”
  • Enjoys relaxing with friends and family, her American Bulldog named Tank, spending time on the beach, snowboarding and surfing, dirt biking and off-roading
  • Considers herself to be a steak connoisseur, foodie, and coffee lover
  • Played soccer as a kid and learned how to snowboard at the age of 4
  • Finished her first 100-mile ride on the back of the tandem with her dad at the age of 9.

bio.education: Ramsay High School University of Alabama
bio.biography:
More

Most Notable

  • Selected AVCA All-America honorable mention as a senior in 2016 at University of Alabama after being named third-team as a sophomore in 2014

Major Competitions
2019: Pan American Games (7th)

Did You Know

  • Named Southeastern Conference Player of the Year in 2016 as a senior at University of Alabama
  • Set University of Alabama school record for career kills (2,148) and career points (2,506)
  • University of Alabama's first AVCA All-American
  • Selected CoSIDA/Capital One Academic All-America Third-Team in 2014
  • Played youth club volleyball for Team Sting Volleyball Club
  • Played volleyball and tennis at Ramsay High School in Birmingham, Alabama, including starting at No. 1 singles and No. 1 doubles
  • Graduated from high school with a 4.48 cumulative grade point average
  • Brother Frank Rivers, Jr., was a quarterback on the Grambling State University football team and played baseball
Career Highlights
  • Pan American Games, Lima, 2019 (Team - Women)

Quick Facts
  • Dom is the son of Liz and Mike Roberto
  • He has one younger sibling, Bella
  • Enjoys playing video games and spending time with friends and family
  • Started diving at age 6
  • His favorite dive is a 5152B
  • His favorite divers are David Boudia and Steele Johnson
  • His favorite TV show is Family Guy and his favorite movie is Ferris Bueller's Day Off
  • Is interested in a career in the medical field
Career Highlights
  • 2017 Junior Pan American Championships team member
  • 2016 junior national champion on platform
  • 2021 Ohio high school state champion (1-meter)

Quick Facts

  • As a black female with a disability, a mental health advocate, and a member of the LGBT+ community, Jaleen Roberts uses her platform to be a catalyst for awareness and change and to show other people that they can succeed despite adversity

  • A favorite quote that speaks to Jaleen is by Henry van Dyke, “Some succeed because they are destined to, but most succeed because they are determined to.” Having been born with cerebral, a condition that impairs muscle strength and coordination caused by a stroke at birth, Jaleen wasn’t destined to succeed but by getting out of her comfort zone and displaying resiliency, she was able to reach one of the highest points of achievement

  • Mental illness is a lifelong battle and Roberts, 24, continues to find her way back to the surface after getting held down by a wave of mental health struggles. As a mental health advocate, she serves as voice for anyone suffering, in hopes of ending the stigma of mental illness within the athletic community. By sharing her story, she hopes to encourage other athletes to reach out and speak up when they are in times of need
Paralympic Experience
  • 2-time Paralympian; 4-time Paralympic medalist (3 silver, 1 bronze)
    • Paralympic Games Paris 2024, silver (Long Jump T37 - Women), bronze (100m T37 - Women), 4th (200m T37 - Women)
    • Paralympic Games Tokyo 2020, silver (100m T37 - Women, Long Jump T37 - Women), 6th (200m T37 - Women)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2023 – (Universal Relay - Mixed), silver (100m T37 - Women, Long Jump T37 - Women), bronze (200m T37 - Women)
  • Years of participation: 100m T37 - Women 2017, 2019, 2023; 200m T37 - Women 2017, 2019, 2023; Long Jump T37 - Women 2017, 2019, 2023; Universal Relay - Mixed 2019, 2023; 400m T37 - Women 2017
  • Medals: 8 (1 gold, 4 silver, 3 bronze)
    • Gold – 2019 (Universal Relay - Mixed)
    • Silver – 2023 (100m T37 - Women, Long Jump T37 - Women); 2019 (Long Jump T37 - Women); 2017 (Long Jump T37 - Women)
    • Bronze – 2023 (200m T37 - Women); 2017 (100m T37 - Women, 200m T37 - Women)

Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Betty Roberts
  • Began riding at age 9, following in the footsteps of her cousin, Brett Banasiewicz, a professional BMX rider
  • Entered her first competition in 2012, after recovering from a fractured vertebra
  • Passionate about growing the sport and increasing women’s participation
  • Biggest inspiration is her future, because “there is so much that can happen and that keeps me going to be my best”
  • Celebrity that would most likely render her speechless is Ellen DeGeneres
  • Pet peeve is bullying in any form
  • Enjoys traveling, filming and editing, and meeting new people
  • Coaches young riders at the skatepark
Olympic Experience
  • 2-time Olympian; 1-time Olympic medalist (1 silver)
    • Olympic Games Paris 2024, 8th (Freestyle Park - Women)
    • Olympic Games Tokyo 2020, silver (Freestyle Park - Women)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2024 – gold (Freestyle Park - Women)
  • Years of participation: Freestyle Park - Women 2017, 2018, 2019, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024
  • Medals: 7 (6 gold, 1 bronze)
    • Gold – 2024 (Freestyle Park - Women); 2023 (Freestyle Park - Women); 2022 (Freestyle Park - Women); 2021 (Freestyle Park - Women); 2019 (Freestyle Park - Women); 2017 (Freestyle Park - Women)
    • Bronze – 2018 (Freestyle Park - Women)
